# Grade 11 student missing after drowning in Kehelgamu Oya
April 4, 2026 10:02 pm 
A schoolboy has gone missing after reportedly drowning in the Kehelgamu Oya, a tributary that feeds into the Castlereagh Reservoir.
The missing student has been identified as a Grade 11 student of Norwood Tamil Maha Vidyalaya.
According to reports, the incident occurred this afternoon (April 4) when the boy, along with several friends, was bathing in an area known as “Silver Wala” in the Kehelgamu Oya in Norwood. He is believed to have drowned and subsequently gone missing.
His friends attempted to rescue him, but their efforts were unsuccessful as the location where he disappeared is said to be a deep section of the river.
The incident was later reported to the Norwood Police.
A search operation has been launched to locate the missing student.

# Sri Lanka rupee closes stronger, bond yields steady
Thursday April 2, 2026 5:10 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 5:10 pm
  * [](http://m.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https://economynext.com/sri-lanka-rupee-closes-stronger-bond-yields-steady-266108/)
  * [](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://economynext.com/sri-lanka-rupee-closes-stronger-bond-yields-steady-266108/)
  * [](https://wa.me/?text=https://economynext.com/sri-lanka-rupee-closes-stronger-bond-yields-steady-266108/)


ECONOMYNEXT – Sri Lanka’s rupee closed at 315.30/40 to the US dollar in the spot market Thursday, from 315.60/80 the previous day, dealers said, while bond yields were broadly steady.
A bond maturing on 15.09.2027 closed flat at 8.90/9.10 percent.
A bond maturing on 01.05.2028 closed at 9.55/70 percent, down from 9.60/70 percent.
A bond maturing on 15.12.2029 closed flat at 9.95/10.00 percent.
A bond maturing on 15.03.2031 closed flat at 10.05/15 percent.
A bond maturing on 15.12.2032 closed flat at 10.60/85 percent.
A bond maturing on 01.06.2033 closed at 11.00/10 percent, down from 11.05/10 percent. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka to develop Rs243bn Maha Oya pumped storage hydro project
Thursday April 2, 2026 4:31 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 4:31 pm
ECONOMYNEXT — Sri Lanka’s cabinet has cleared a proposal for the Ceylon Electricity Board (CEB) to develop the Maha Oya pumped storage hydropower project at a cost of 243.09 billion rupees, minister Nalinda Jayatissa said.
The project is designed to act as a grid-scale energy storage solution to store excess energy generated from renewable sources. 
“It is expected to enable the storage of energy during periods of low demand and the supply of such stored energy to the power system during periods of high demand,” Jayatissa told reporters.
The project is essential for integrating more renewable energy into the country’s power mix, he said.
The project will have a development phase followed by a construction phase.
Funding for the 243.09 billion rupee initiative will be managed under the provisions of the Public Debt Management Act No. 33 of 2024, Jayatissa said. 
Pumped storage technology is increasingly seen as a vital tool for Sri Lanka as it shifts away from fossil fuels. 
It functions like a giant battery, using surplus electricity to pump water to an upper reservoir, which is later released through turbines to generate power when the grid faces a deficit.
The Maha Oya project is expected to significantly improve grid stability and reduce the reliance on expensive thermal power plants during peak evening hours. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka stocks close up 0.43-pct, bank stocks push turnover
Thursday April 2, 2026 3:48 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 3:48 pm
ECONOMYNEXT – The Colombo Stock Exchange All Share Price Index closed up 0.43 percent on Tuesday, while the S&P SL20 fell 0.26 percent, CSE data showed.
The ASPI was up 89.92 points at 21,156.10.
The S&P SL20 was down 15.62 points at 5,888.48.
Hatton National Bank and Commercial Bank saw a considerable fall in points as their dividends expired, resulting in the S&P SL20 index falling, analysts said.
Melstacorp (up 1.64 percent at 170.75 rupees), Sampath Bank (up 0.86 percent at 146.50 rupees), R I L Properties (up 2.79 percent at 25.80 rupees) were the top positive contributors to ASPI.
Hatton National Bank (down 1.83 percent at 399.50 rupees), Central Finance Company (down 1.97 percent at 223.50 rupees), LOLC Holdings (down 1.89 percent at 5.20 rupees) were the top negative contributors.
Turnover was 3.57 billion rupees. Capital goods contributed 452 million rupees.
Commercial Credit and Finance announced an interim dividend of 3 rupees per share. The share closed up 3.86 percent at 114.25 rupees.
People’s Leasing & Finance, a subsidiary of Sri Lanka’s People’s Bank, said it had completed a 5-year term loan facility of 10 billion rupees through an issue of trust certificates on private placement basis. The share closed up 0.45 percent at 22.50 rupees.
In global markets, India’s Nifty50 closed up 0.065 percent at 22,694.25 points. Hong Kong’s Hang Seng saw a fall of 0.70 percent at 25,116.53 points. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Thailand corporate, YWCA gift Sri Lanka Rs5mn for post-Ditwah recovery
Thursday April 2, 2026 3:02 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 3:02 pm
ECONOMYNEXT — Thailand’s private sector and civil society are supporting Sri Lanka’s reconstruction efforts, with YWCA Bangkok and Thai Beverage Public Company Limited (ThaiBev) donating around 5 million rupees (approx 26,000 dollars).
YWCA Bangkok contributed 200,000 Thai baht, while the Thai Beverage Public Company Limited contributed 10,000 American dollars to boost the ‘Sri Lanka government’s Humanitarian Priorities Plan (HPP).
The funds were handed over to Sri Lanka’s Ambassador and Permanent Representative to Thailand Wijayanthi Edirisinghe at the Embassy in Bangkok. 
The YWCA contribution was presented by its President, Lantip Dvadasin, while the ThaiBev donation was delivered by Chamapan Rangratna, Advisor to the CEO of Corporate Affairs.
The move follows a broader trend of Thai-Sri Lankan cooperation. Earlier in January 2026, the Royal Thai Government provided a direct financial grant of 10.2 million rupees for post-cyclone reconstruction.
“The Embassy of Sri Lanka reiterates its sincere gratitude to the members of YWCA Bangkok, and Thai Beverage Public Company Limited, for their timely and generous support during this challenging time,” the embassy [said](https://bangkok.embassy.gov.lk/ywca-bangkok-and-thaibev-contribute-to-cyclone-ditwah-relief-efforts-in-sri-lanka/). (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka People’s Leasing strengthens capital through Rs10bn private placement
Thursday April 2, 2026 1:46 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 1:46 pm
ECONOMYNEXT – People’s Leasing & Finance, a subsidiary of Sri Lanka’s People’s Bank, said it had completed a 5-year term loan facility of 10 billion rupees through an issue of trust certificates on private placement basis.
The unlisted, unrated, unsecured, subordinated facility is meant for strengthening the company’s Tier 2 capital, it said in a stock exchange [filing](https://cdn.cse.lk/cmt/announcement_portal_prod/Amended%20CSE%20Disclosure%20on%20Trust%20Certificates_48902490822663773.pdf).
“This strategic initiative strengthened the Company’s capital base and supported its growth plan while ensuring the Capital Adequacy requirement of the Company.”
A central bank had approved including the funds under Tier 2 capital of the company in terms of the Finance Business Act (Capital Adequacy Requirements) Direction No. 3 of 2018. (COLOMBO/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka to implement water cuts to manage supply
Thursday April 2, 2026 12:12 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 12:12 pm
ECONOMYNEXT – The National Water Supply and Drainage Board has said its water sources are running low, and they are looking to avert an acute problem by limiting supply to the island’s Western Province immediately. 
“From tomorrow, we will have to limit water supply to places that have so far received 24-hour water supply,” NWSDB chairman Chandana Bandara told reporters.
“We anticipate problems arising in the next two weeks, especially with the upcoming holiday season, so we need to manage water supply.”
If the NWSDB continues supplying water at its current capacity, water at its Kalatuwawa Reservoir will be sufficient only for 20 days, he said.
“With the prevailing hot weather, the demand for water is going up. We have to limit water supply to avoid a worst case scenario.”
If it rains within the next 2-3 weeks, he said, the problem can be managed better.
“If it doesn’t rain and these drought conditions worsen, we can’t implement a plan then when the water has all gone.” 
![](https://economynext.com/sri-lanka-rupee-closes-stronger-bond-yields-steady-266108/)
People living at higher elevations and those on upper floors are seeing a loss in water pressure even now, and this will get worse, he said. 
The NWSDB took all this into consideration and decided to impose water cuts to ensure everyone gets water, he said.
Bowsers will be used to transport water to areas not getting water.
“Consumers will be inconvenienced but this water management has to be done in order to avert a crisis. We ask for your support in helping us manage the situation.” 
As an essential service, NWSDB employees’ leaves have been cancelled he said. 
The NWSDB supplies 2.5 million cubic metres of water daily, its director Lalith Wijesinghe said, with 50 percent of this, or 1.25 million cubic metres, coming from the Western Province where water levels are running low at both its sources, rivers and reservoirs. 
About 700,000 cubic metres of water go to meet Colombo District’s water demand, 450,000 to Gampaha District; and 1850,000 to Kalutara District. 
“As of today we are still distributing these quantities, but we have had to control it,” Wijesinghe said. 
Colombo is supplied by the Ambatale, Labugama and Kalatuwawa water treatment plants. 
The water levels of the Kelani River, which supplies the Ambatale plant, has dropped significantly raising the risk of a salinity problem, Wijesinghe said, in response to which a barrier has been implemented.
“We are facing an acute problem with the Labugama and Kalatuwawa water treatment plants, so we have problems with areas of Colombo supplied by these plants.” 
Other regions of the island, especially those supplied by the Mahaweli region are ok for now, as water levels are at around 50-60 percent, Bandara said. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka stocks trend up, banks push turnover
Thursday April 2, 2026 11:23 am
Thursday April 2, 2026 11:23 am
ECONOMYNEXT – Sri Lanka’s Colombo Stock Exchange opened up on Thursday, with bank stocks leading turnover. 
The All Share Price Index was up 0.55 percent, or 116.65 points, at 21,182.83.
However, the S&P SL20 was down 0.25 percent, or 14.5 points, at 5,889.60.
Top positive contributors were Sampath Bank (up 1.38 percent at Rs.147.25), ACL Cables (up 4.71 percent at 89 rupees) and Dialog Axiata (up 1.98 percent at 30.90 rupees).
Hatton National Bank (down 1.77 percent at 399.75 rupees) and John Keells Holdings (down 0.54 percent at 18.30 rupees) were the top negative contributors to ASPI.
Brent futures climbed back up over 100 dollars after a 2-day decline, amidst US President Trump’s claims of bombing Iran “back to the stone ages”.
Market turnover was 370 million rupees. Bank stocks led turnover with 96.96 million rupees.
Sri Lanka energy firm WindForce said it plans to issue up to 40 million green bonds to raise 4 billion rupees.
The company will issue up to 40 million listed, rated, senior, unsecured, redeemable green bonds, the company said in a stock exchange filing. The share was up 1.78 percent at 45.80 rupees. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Prime Minister highlights resilience and hope in Easter message
by [Supuni Kulasinghe](https://dailynews.lk/author/supuni/) April 5, 2026
April 5, 2026 8:21 am   
  

[ ![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20768'%3E%3C/svg%3E) ](https://dailynews.lk/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/efrt5y46ujy.jpg)
_34_
Prime Minister Harini Amarasuriya has extended Easter greetings to Christian devotees in Sri Lanka and around the world, wishing them a blessed Easter filled with peace, happiness and blessings.
In her message marking Easter Sunday, the Prime Minister said the resurrection of Jesus Christ conveys the message that light can overcome darkness, faith can conquer fear and happiness can triumph over suffering.
She noted that although people face various challenges, hardships and obstacles in life, the message of Easter reminds humanity that darkness does not last forever and that hope always follows difficult times.
Prime Minister Amarasuriya also referred to the suffering experienced by many people due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, expressing hope for the dawn of a peaceful world where dialogue and understanding prevail over hatred and conflict.
Calling on people to be guided by the message of love and peace preached by Christ, she urged the public to nurture love, patience and forgiveness while working together in unity to build Sri Lanka as one family founded on brotherhood and peace.

# Sri Lanka to develop Rs243bn Maha Oya pumped storage hydro project
Thursday April 2, 2026 4:31 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 4:31 pm
  * [](http://m.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https://economynext.com/sri-lanka-to-develop-rs243bn-maha-oya-pumped-storage-hydro-project-266090/)
  * [](https://twitter.com/share?url=https://economynext.com/sri-lanka-to-develop-rs243bn-maha-oya-pumped-storage-hydro-project-266090/)
  * [](https://wa.me/?text=https://economynext.com/sri-lanka-to-develop-rs243bn-maha-oya-pumped-storage-hydro-project-266090/)


ECONOMYNEXT — Sri Lanka’s cabinet has cleared a proposal for the Ceylon Electricity Board (CEB) to develop the Maha Oya pumped storage hydropower project at a cost of 243.09 billion rupees, minister Nalinda Jayatissa said.
The project is designed to act as a grid-scale energy storage solution to store excess energy generated from renewable sources. 
“It is expected to enable the storage of energy during periods of low demand and the supply of such stored energy to the power system during periods of high demand,” Jayatissa told reporters.
The project is essential for integrating more renewable energy into the country’s power mix, he said.
The project will have a development phase followed by a construction phase.
Funding for the 243.09 billion rupee initiative will be managed under the provisions of the Public Debt Management Act No. 33 of 2024, Jayatissa said. 
Pumped storage technology is increasingly seen as a vital tool for Sri Lanka as it shifts away from fossil fuels. 
It functions like a giant battery, using surplus electricity to pump water to an upper reservoir, which is later released through turbines to generate power when the grid faces a deficit.
The Maha Oya project is expected to significantly improve grid stability and reduce the reliance on expensive thermal power plants during peak evening hours. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka stocks close up 0.43-pct, bank stocks push turnover
Thursday April 2, 2026 3:48 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 3:48 pm
ECONOMYNEXT – The Colombo Stock Exchange All Share Price Index closed up 0.43 percent on Tuesday, while the S&P SL20 fell 0.26 percent, CSE data showed.
The ASPI was up 89.92 points at 21,156.10.
The S&P SL20 was down 15.62 points at 5,888.48.
Hatton National Bank and Commercial Bank saw a considerable fall in points as their dividends expired, resulting in the S&P SL20 index falling, analysts said.
Melstacorp (up 1.64 percent at 170.75 rupees), Sampath Bank (up 0.86 percent at 146.50 rupees), R I L Properties (up 2.79 percent at 25.80 rupees) were the top positive contributors to ASPI.
Hatton National Bank (down 1.83 percent at 399.50 rupees), Central Finance Company (down 1.97 percent at 223.50 rupees), LOLC Holdings (down 1.89 percent at 5.20 rupees) were the top negative contributors.
Turnover was 3.57 billion rupees. Capital goods contributed 452 million rupees.
Commercial Credit and Finance announced an interim dividend of 3 rupees per share. The share closed up 3.86 percent at 114.25 rupees.
People’s Leasing & Finance, a subsidiary of Sri Lanka’s People’s Bank, said it had completed a 5-year term loan facility of 10 billion rupees through an issue of trust certificates on private placement basis. The share closed up 0.45 percent at 22.50 rupees.
In global markets, India’s Nifty50 closed up 0.065 percent at 22,694.25 points. Hong Kong’s Hang Seng saw a fall of 0.70 percent at 25,116.53 points. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Thailand corporate, YWCA gift Sri Lanka Rs5mn for post-Ditwah recovery
Thursday April 2, 2026 3:02 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 3:02 pm
ECONOMYNEXT — Thailand’s private sector and civil society are supporting Sri Lanka’s reconstruction efforts, with YWCA Bangkok and Thai Beverage Public Company Limited (ThaiBev) donating around 5 million rupees (approx 26,000 dollars).
YWCA Bangkok contributed 200,000 Thai baht, while the Thai Beverage Public Company Limited contributed 10,000 American dollars to boost the ‘Sri Lanka government’s Humanitarian Priorities Plan (HPP).
The funds were handed over to Sri Lanka’s Ambassador and Permanent Representative to Thailand Wijayanthi Edirisinghe at the Embassy in Bangkok. 
The YWCA contribution was presented by its President, Lantip Dvadasin, while the ThaiBev donation was delivered by Chamapan Rangratna, Advisor to the CEO of Corporate Affairs.
The move follows a broader trend of Thai-Sri Lankan cooperation. Earlier in January 2026, the Royal Thai Government provided a direct financial grant of 10.2 million rupees for post-cyclone reconstruction.
“The Embassy of Sri Lanka reiterates its sincere gratitude to the members of YWCA Bangkok, and Thai Beverage Public Company Limited, for their timely and generous support during this challenging time,” the embassy [said](https://bangkok.embassy.gov.lk/ywca-bangkok-and-thaibev-contribute-to-cyclone-ditwah-relief-efforts-in-sri-lanka/). (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka People’s Leasing strengthens capital through Rs10bn private placement
Thursday April 2, 2026 1:46 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 1:46 pm
ECONOMYNEXT – People’s Leasing & Finance, a subsidiary of Sri Lanka’s People’s Bank, said it had completed a 5-year term loan facility of 10 billion rupees through an issue of trust certificates on private placement basis.
The unlisted, unrated, unsecured, subordinated facility is meant for strengthening the company’s Tier 2 capital, it said in a stock exchange [filing](https://cdn.cse.lk/cmt/announcement_portal_prod/Amended%20CSE%20Disclosure%20on%20Trust%20Certificates_48902490822663773.pdf).
“This strategic initiative strengthened the Company’s capital base and supported its growth plan while ensuring the Capital Adequacy requirement of the Company.”
A central bank had approved including the funds under Tier 2 capital of the company in terms of the Finance Business Act (Capital Adequacy Requirements) Direction No. 3 of 2018. (COLOMBO/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka to implement water cuts to manage supply
Thursday April 2, 2026 12:12 pm
Thursday April 2, 2026 12:12 pm
ECONOMYNEXT – The National Water Supply and Drainage Board has said its water sources are running low, and they are looking to avert an acute problem by limiting supply to the island’s Western Province immediately. 
“From tomorrow, we will have to limit water supply to places that have so far received 24-hour water supply,” NWSDB chairman Chandana Bandara told reporters.
“We anticipate problems arising in the next two weeks, especially with the upcoming holiday season, so we need to manage water supply.”
If the NWSDB continues supplying water at its current capacity, water at its Kalatuwawa Reservoir will be sufficient only for 20 days, he said.
“With the prevailing hot weather, the demand for water is going up. We have to limit water supply to avoid a worst case scenario.”
If it rains within the next 2-3 weeks, he said, the problem can be managed better.
“If it doesn’t rain and these drought conditions worsen, we can’t implement a plan then when the water has all gone.” 
![](https://economynext.com/sri-lanka-to-develop-rs243bn-maha-oya-pumped-storage-hydro-project-266090/)
People living at higher elevations and those on upper floors are seeing a loss in water pressure even now, and this will get worse, he said. 
The NWSDB took all this into consideration and decided to impose water cuts to ensure everyone gets water, he said.
Bowsers will be used to transport water to areas not getting water.
“Consumers will be inconvenienced but this water management has to be done in order to avert a crisis. We ask for your support in helping us manage the situation.” 
As an essential service, NWSDB employees’ leaves have been cancelled he said. 
The NWSDB supplies 2.5 million cubic metres of water daily, its director Lalith Wijesinghe said, with 50 percent of this, or 1.25 million cubic metres, coming from the Western Province where water levels are running low at both its sources, rivers and reservoirs. 
About 700,000 cubic metres of water go to meet Colombo District’s water demand, 450,000 to Gampaha District; and 1850,000 to Kalutara District. 
“As of today we are still distributing these quantities, but we have had to control it,” Wijesinghe said. 
Colombo is supplied by the Ambatale, Labugama and Kalatuwawa water treatment plants. 
The water levels of the Kelani River, which supplies the Ambatale plant, has dropped significantly raising the risk of a salinity problem, Wijesinghe said, in response to which a barrier has been implemented.
“We are facing an acute problem with the Labugama and Kalatuwawa water treatment plants, so we have problems with areas of Colombo supplied by these plants.” 
Other regions of the island, especially those supplied by the Mahaweli region are ok for now, as water levels are at around 50-60 percent, Bandara said. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka stocks trend up, banks push turnover
Thursday April 2, 2026 11:23 am
Thursday April 2, 2026 11:23 am
ECONOMYNEXT – Sri Lanka’s Colombo Stock Exchange opened up on Thursday, with bank stocks leading turnover. 
The All Share Price Index was up 0.55 percent, or 116.65 points, at 21,182.83.
However, the S&P SL20 was down 0.25 percent, or 14.5 points, at 5,889.60.
Top positive contributors were Sampath Bank (up 1.38 percent at Rs.147.25), ACL Cables (up 4.71 percent at 89 rupees) and Dialog Axiata (up 1.98 percent at 30.90 rupees).
Hatton National Bank (down 1.77 percent at 399.75 rupees) and John Keells Holdings (down 0.54 percent at 18.30 rupees) were the top negative contributors to ASPI.
Brent futures climbed back up over 100 dollars after a 2-day decline, amidst US President Trump’s claims of bombing Iran “back to the stone ages”.
Market turnover was 370 million rupees. Bank stocks led turnover with 96.96 million rupees.
Sri Lanka energy firm WindForce said it plans to issue up to 40 million green bonds to raise 4 billion rupees.
The company will issue up to 40 million listed, rated, senior, unsecured, redeemable green bonds, the company said in a stock exchange filing. The share was up 1.78 percent at 45.80 rupees. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

# Sri Lanka’s Windforce to raise Rs4bn in green bond issue
Thursday April 2, 2026 10:34 am
Thursday April 2, 2026 10:34 am
ECONOMYNEXT – Sri Lanka energy firm WindForce Plc said it plans to issue up to 40 million green bonds to raise 4 billion rupees.
The company will issue up to 40 million listed, rated, senior, unsecured, redeemable green bonds, the company said in a [stock exchange filing](https://cdn.cse.lk/cmt/announcement_portal_prod/02.04.2026%20-%20%20Greenbond_48890725190050480.pdf).
“The details on the tenure, interest rates and other characteristics of the Green Bond issue will be notified in due course.”
The issue is subject to necessary regulatory approvals. 
Last month WindForce was awarded a contract to build a standalone Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) project in Panadura, with a capacity of 10 MW/40 MWh, at a cost of 1.73 billion rupees.
The total investment for the BESS project is estimated at 1.73 billion rupees, structured on an 80:20 debt‑to‑equity financing model.
With this, WindForce’s cumulative portfolio under the national BESS procurement program increased to 13 standalone BESS projects. (Colombo/Apr2/2026)

![](https://bmkltsly13vb.compat.objectstorage.ap-singapore-1.oraclecloud.com/cdn.sg.dailymirror.lk/assets/uploads/image_146409032d.jpg)
Colombo, April 1 (Daily Mirror) - Sri Lanka has secured fuel supplies from Russia, the Ceylon Petroleum Corporation (CPC) stated.
CPC Managing Director Dr. Mayura Neththikumara stated that delays in the arrival of fuel shipments are due to congestion at the country’s two main fuel unloading terminals.
Explaining the situation, Dr. Neththikumara said discussions with Russian authorities are ongoing, and fuel shipments for April and May have largely been confirmed.
He further noted that Sri Lanka currently has only two primary locations capable of handling fuel unloading, and logistical and technical arrangements — including scheduling the arrival of vessels from Russian suppliers — are still being finalised.
However, he emphasised that securing fuel supplies from Russia has already been confirmed.

# Even after Iran war ends, restoring global economy will take two years or more – Analyst
April 4, 2026 05:00 pm 
Discover more
Water
Vehicles
VPN services
Multimedia News Platform
Digital media equipment
Geographic Reference
Email Newsletters
Mobile phone accessories
Local artisan crafts
Sri Lankan Products
Political analyst Dr. Tudor Weerasinghe has warned that ongoing military conflicts in the Middle East could have severe repercussions for Sri Lanka and other Asian countries.
He noted that the countries involved have shown neither the need nor the willingness to end the conflict, which could push the region into a deep crisis.
Dr. Weerasinghe highlighted that Israel and the United States have already carried out airstrikes against Iran, and 36 days into the conflict, its evolving dynamics continue to raise concerns.
Speaking to the media, he said that Asian countries will face significant challenges, and domestic resistance within these nations is likely to increase. Social and political unrest within these countries could escalate, and Sri Lanka, as part of the region, may also face similar pressures.
Dr. Weerasinghe added that the conflict has already triggered an economic crisis that is felt globally, and if it continues, it could escalate into a full-blown economic downturn.
“Due to this war, disruptions in global supply chains and key energy hubs are already being felt. If the conflict continues beyond 36 days, Asian countries such as Sri Lanka, Bangladesh, and Singapore, which heavily depend on Middle Eastern oil, will face even greater impacts. This will not only intensify economic resistance within these countries but also heighten social and political unrest. Maintaining state and government authority under such conditions could become a major challenge,” he said.
Dr. Tudor Weerasinghe also warned that even if the conflict stops in the coming days, it will take at least two years for the global economy to fully recover.  
“Even after the war ends, restoring the global economy will take around two years or more. The impact of the COVID-19 pandemic has already set back global economic recovery, and the current situation could multiply these challenges several times over in the coming months.”
